beats.description: Luke and Amira enter the street in profile and begin a walk-and-talk phrase
continuityNotes: zone/practical emphasis only; no new channels introduced

beats.description: They cross the window-lamp spill and Amira takes the line

beats.description: They pause near the fire basket and Luke answers in a lower register
continuityNotes: zone/practical emphasis only; no new channels introduced

This is currently the highest contiguous ready band and carries full-program promotion responsibility.

topConcerns: At least one tracked change class reopens the rollout from B1.
All bands are promotable, but promotion still depends on maintaining the current artifact set.

programRisks.summary: The rollout graph has a single root package: B1-adapter-contract-loaders.
programRisks.trigger: Any slip in the first adapter package delays the entire program.
mitigationActions: Keep the initial adapter contract intentionally narrow.
Pre-stage validation and fixture maintenance work so the first band gets fast feedback.

programRisks.summary: The combined engine currently has a long serial chain of 16 packages.
programRisks.trigger: Small slips compound because downstream bands start only after prior chain segments clear.
mitigationActions: Preserve early parallel-relief work so validation and fixture tracks stay productive.
Keep each band scoped to the minimum proof needed for the next band.

programRisks.summary: There are too few clearly parallel relief packages early in the program.
programRisks.trigger: Teams may idle while waiting for the critical-path chain to unblock.
mitigationActions: Pre-assign fixture, audit, and documentation upkeep as contingency work.
Convert any non-blocking validation package into a continuously runnable gate.

riskRegister.summary: B1-adapter-contract-loaders sits at critical-path position 1 with 17 downstream packages relying on it.
mitigationActions: Freeze the adapter contract early and keep a minimal compatibility shim available.
Promote schema and fixture checks before runtime wiring expands.
Protect focus time for adapter-track while this package is active.
Use support tracks in B1 for fixture and audit maintenance: fixture-track.
contingencyWork: Advance same-band support work: B1-validation-contract-gate.
Keep global parallel-relief packages moving: B1-validation-contract-gate.
Expand fixture coverage and diff outputs so downstream validation can start immediately once the blocker clears.

riskRegister.summary: B1-runtime-fixture-harness sits at critical-path position 2 with 15 downstream packages relying on it.

Protect focus time for runtime-track while this package is active.

bandPlaybooks.adapterFallback: Keep compatibility adapters and schema guards moving inside B1 while the main adapter blocker clears.
bandPlaybooks.runtimeFallback: Advance deterministic harness work and non-blocking playback probes inside B1 without widening scope.
bandPlaybooks.validationFallback: Keep regression gates, audits, and acceptance checks warm for B1 so blocked work can re-enter quickly.
bandPlaybooks.fixtureFallback: Expand fixtures, examples, and handoff coverage for B1 so downstream teams stay unblocked.

trackFallbacks.validation-track: Convert partial artifacts into continuously runnable gates so quality checks do not stall.
trackFallbacks.fixture-track: Expand fixtures, examples, and handoff artifacts so restart cost stays low.
trackFallbacks.runtime-track: Keep deterministic harnesses minimal and avoid branching into new scene classes while blocked.
trackFallbacks.adapter-track: Hold contract shape stable, narrow scope, and advance schema guards.

globalContingencies: Protect the first adapter and runtime packages from side work while they remain on the critical path.
Use validation-track and fixture-track as the default relief valves whenever a runtime or adapter package slips.
Do not widen scope inside a blocked band; only advance already-declared fallback work.
